title: SDLC Artifact Factory — Product Configuration
description: Product configuration collected by the /sdlc-start interactive questionnaire. Saved to sdlc-config.json and consumed by all skills and agents for this product run.

product_name.description: Product codename used in artifact headers, repo names, and the manifest. Lowercase, hyphen-separated (e.g. data-estate-map).

problem_statement.description: The full problem statement provided at /sdlc-start. Used as context by all skills.

target_language.description: Primary programming language for generated code artifacts.

compliance_frameworks.description: Compliance frameworks in scope for this product. Drives compliance-design, compliance-test-spec, and compliance-assessor artifacts.

tenancy_model.description: How the product handles multiple customers/tenants.

cloud_providers.description: Cloud providers the IaC and deployment artifacts must support.

graph_database.description: Graph database to use. 'none' if the product does not require a graph store.

mongodb_hybrid.description: Whether MongoDB is used as an optional document store for variable-schema bounded contexts (e.g. entity extraction output, crawl metadata).

sensitive_data_types.description: Categories of sensitive data this product handles. Drives data-classification, privacy-design, and compliance artifacts.

repo_strategy.description: Repository structure. multi-repo is the default; repos are derived from bounded context mapping.

github_org.description: GitHub organisation or user under which product repos will be created (e.g. acme-corp). Required for multi-repo strategy.

nl_querying.description: Whether the product includes natural language querying via a locally hosted LLM. Adds infrastructure and model hosting requirements to the deployment architecture.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this config was created.
